title: Complete Remaining Development Plan Tasks for AI Code Knowledge MCP System
overview: The AI Code Knowledge System is a TypeScript project that extracts structured knowledge (symbol graphs, call graphs, dependency graphs, LLM file summaries) from a codebase and exposes it to Claude Code via the Model Context Protocol (MCP). Phases 1–7 are fully implemented: symbol extraction via ts-morph, dependency graph building with cycle detection, LLM summary caching with SHA-256 hashing, atomic knowledge storage, an MCP server with 6 tools (find_symbol, find_callers, get_dependencies, get_file_summary, search_architecture, health_check), and a chokidar file-watcher for sub-second incremental updates. The remaining work—which this PRD captures—covers Phase 8 (Claude Code MCP integration via .claude/ config files that were blocked by sandbox restrictions) and Phase 9 (full test suite execution, end-to-end validation, MCP server compilation verification), plus two minor code-quality improvements identified in the QA report. Completing these tasks brings the project from approximately 95% complete to fully production-ready.
goals: Unblock Phase 8 by creating the .claude/mcp_servers.json and .claude/mcp_servers.dev.json configuration files that register the knowledge MCP server with Claude Code, enabling it to be automatically launched in Claude sessions
Complete Phase 9 validation by compiling the MCP server TypeScript to CommonJS, running the full 11-file Vitest test suite, and confirming the 500-file performance benchmark finishes under 10 seconds
Resolve all critical and major issues in the QA report: missing .claude config files and unverified mcp-server/dist/ compilation output
Apply two minor code-quality fixes (logging inconsistency in get-file-summary.ts, grammatically incorrect function name in watch.ts) to align the codebase with its own internal conventions
Verify end-to-end correctness: knowledge build pipeline produces all four artifacts, and each MCP tool returns accurate results when queried against the test fixture knowledge base

requirements.description: Create the file .claude/mcp_servers.json at the project root containing a valid JSON object that registers the production MCP server: { "mcpServers": { "knowledge": { "command": "node", "args": ["mcp-server/dist/server.js"], "env": { "KNOWLEDGE_ROOT": ".knowledge" } } } }. The .claude/ directory must be created if it does not already exist.

Create the file .claude/mcp_servers.dev.json at the project root containing a valid JSON object that registers the development MCP server: { "mcpServers": { "knowledge": { "command": "npx", "args": ["tsx", "mcp-server/server.ts"], "env": { "KNOWLEDGE_ROOT": ".knowledge" } } } }. This config runs TypeScript directly via tsx without requiring a prior build step.

Both .claude/mcp_servers.json and .claude/mcp_servers.dev.json must be parseable by JSON.parse() without errors, must follow the Claude Code MCP server configuration schema (mcpServers.<name>.command/args/env), and must include the KNOWLEDGE_ROOT environment variable set to '.knowledge' (relative to CWD).

Running 'npm run build-mcp' must succeed with exit code 0. The prebuild-mcp lifecycle script must first delete mcp-server/dist/ (if it exists) to eliminate stale artifacts. After the build, mcp-server/dist/server.js must exist and be a valid CommonJS module.

After compilation, executing 'node mcp-server/dist/server.js' must: (a) start without throwing any synchronous or asynchronous startup errors, (b) produce zero bytes of output to stdout before any MCP request is received, (c) remain alive and listening on stdin rather than exiting immediately.

The full Vitest test suite ('npm test') must complete with exit code 0 and zero failing tests across all 11 test files: symbol-extractor.test.ts, dependency-graph.test.ts, find-symbol.test.ts, find-callers.test.ts, get-dependencies.test.ts, get-file-summary.test.ts, health-check.test.ts, search-architecture.test.ts, index-builder.test.ts, mcp-tools.test.ts, and perf.test.ts.

The MCP tool integration test suite (test/mcp-tools.test.ts) must successfully execute the full knowledge build pipeline against test/fixtures/sample-project in its beforeAll hook, invoke all 6 MCP tool handlers with representative inputs, assert correct output content, and remove the generated .knowledge directory in its afterAll cleanup hook.

The find_symbol integration test must return a result for the query 'createOrder' that includes the symbol name and a reference to the order-service source file, confirming the symbol extraction and indexing pipeline is working correctly end-to-end.

The find_callers integration test must return a result for the query 'charge' that identifies 'createOrder' as a caller, confirming the call graph inversion (calledBy index) is correctly built and queryable.

The get_dependencies integration test must return actual module names (not the string 'undefined') in its formatted output for the 'sample-project' module, confirming that the TASK-002 bug fix (using result.to instead of result.dependency) is applied and operational.

The health_check integration test must return a formatted string that contains each of these fields: 'Last Built', 'File Count', 'Has Symbols', 'Has Dependencies', and 'Modules', confirming that .knowledge/index.json is read and rendered correctly.

The performance benchmark test (test/perf.test.ts) must confirm that 'npm run build-knowledge' completes in under 10 seconds when run against a 500-file TypeScript project using the static summarizer (no external LLM API calls).

Running 'npm run build-knowledge' against test/fixtures/sample-project must produce all four expected knowledge artifacts at non-zero sizes: .knowledge/symbols.json, .knowledge/dependencies.json, .knowledge/summaries/cache.json, and .knowledge/index.json. All four files must be valid JSON.

In mcp-server/tools/get-file-summary.ts, replace the console.error() call with process.stderr.write() to match the logging convention used throughout the rest of the codebase (server.ts, watch.ts, build-knowledge.ts all use process.stderr.write exclusively). This is a one-line change with no behavioral impact.

In scripts/watch.ts, rename the function 'scheduleFlushed' to 'scheduleFlush' at both its declaration site and its call site (approximately line 64 and its usage). This is a cosmetic active-verb naming correction with no behavioral impact.

Add a code comment in scripts/lib/incremental-updater.ts near the ts-morph Project instantiation (where skipFileDependencyResolution: true is set) explicitly documenting the known trade-off: single-file incremental updates complete in under 1 second but may result in stale calledBy data for cross-file call edges, as symbols in other files that call the changed file's symbols will not be re-resolved.

TypeScript type checking must pass with zero errors across all source files in src/, scripts/, and mcp-server/ directories. This can be verified with 'tsc --noEmit' or equivalent, and must hold after all code-quality fixes are applied.

All MCP tool error conditions (missing knowledge files, parse failures, invalid input arguments) must write diagnostic messages exclusively to stderr. No error text, stack traces, or diagnostic logs must appear on stdout, as stdout is reserved exclusively for MCP protocol JSON messages.

constraints: The .claude/ directory is treated as a sensitive path by the Claude Code agent sandbox; files inside it cannot be created or modified by Claude agents and must be created manually by the project owner or via an operator-level script executed outside the sandbox environment
The MCP server must use @modelcontextprotocol/sdk with StdioServerTransport exclusively — no HTTP ports or WebSocket connections — to be compatible with the Claude Code MCP subprocess launch model

The MCP server TypeScript source must compile to CommonJS output (not ESM) via tsconfig.mcp.json so that the resulting server.js can be executed with plain 'node' without requiring --experimental-vm-modules or import assertion flags
No new npm runtime dependencies may be added; only devDependencies additions are permissible if strictly required for test infrastructure that does not already exist

Existing source files from Phases 1–7 must not be modified except for the two targeted code-quality fixes described in REQ-014 (get-file-summary.ts logging) and REQ-015 (watch.ts function naming); all core Phase 1–7 implementation is considered stable

The KNOWLEDGE_ROOT environment variable must default to '.knowledge' (relative to the process CWD) when not explicitly set, ensuring the MCP server functions correctly without any environment configuration in simple use cases
Test execution and npm build commands may require interactive sandbox approval; all test verification must therefore be performed in an environment with appropriate shell execution permissions

The build pipeline must remain idempotent: running 'npm run build-knowledge' twice on an unchanged project must produce byte-for-byte identical output artifacts and must not make LLM API calls for files whose SHA-256 content hash is already present in the summary cache
acceptance_criteria: .claude/mcp_servers.json exists, is parseable JSON, and specifies command='node' with args=['mcp-server/dist/server.js'] and env.KNOWLEDGE_ROOT='.knowledge'
.claude/mcp_servers.dev.json exists, is parseable JSON, and specifies command='npx' with args=['tsx', 'mcp-server/server.ts'] and env.KNOWLEDGE_ROOT='.knowledge'
'npm run build-mcp' exits with code 0 and produces mcp-server/dist/server.js
'node mcp-server/dist/server.js' starts without error, produces no stdout output before a request, and stays alive awaiting stdin
'npm test' exits with code 0 with all 11 test files reporting zero failures
The perf benchmark confirms knowledge build time for 500 TypeScript files is under 10 seconds in static summarizer mode

The mcp-tools.test.ts integration tests verify: find_symbol('createOrder') returns the correct file path, find_callers('charge') returns createOrder as a caller, get_dependencies returns real module names (not 'undefined'), get_file_summary returns a non-error result for order-service.ts, health_check returns a string containing 'Last Built' and 'File Count', and search_architecture exercises both the match-found and no-results code paths
'npm run build-knowledge' produces all four valid non-empty JSON artifacts: .knowledge/symbols.json, .knowledge/dependencies.json, .knowledge/summaries/cache.json, and .knowledge/index.json
mcp-server/tools/get-file-summary.ts uses process.stderr.write() (not console.error()) for its error logging line
scripts/watch.ts uses the function name 'scheduleFlush' (not 'scheduleFlushed') at both declaration and call site
TypeScript type checking passes with zero errors across all project source files
No MCP protocol stdout output is produced by the compiled server before the first client request is received
